observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the: 

9. GSO to prioritize and prepare the IIRUP, including the identification of methods of disposal for the above unserviceable equipment and the conduct of disposal, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386 and COA-DBM 2024-01 to avoid further deterioration of these equipment which would eventually affects the saleable value, and submit the approved IIRUP, official Receipts, if any and other documents to the Municipal Accountant; and
10. Municipal Accountant to derecognize the disposed PPEs from the books, after actual disposal of the unserviceable properties, and upon receipt of the IIRUP and other documents from the GSO.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed:

11. To file the request for authority to write-off dormant loan receivables and direct the Municipal Accountant to support the request for write-off of dormant loan receivables with pertinent documents following the procedures in COA Circular No. 2016-005;

13. Together with the Municipal Accountant, and Investigation Committee to initiate the procedures for the proper disposition and derecognition of dormant receivable accounts amounting to P341,183.16 as provided in Items 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular.

23. Prior to business renewal period, gather Alphalist of Payees subjected to Expanded Withholding Tax and/or List of Purchases procured by the Municipality and/or Barangays from business establishments registered under their jurisdiction, and upon receipt of business renewal applications, conduct verification and compare the gross sales declared in the sworn statement of business establishments to ensure that the declared gross sales are true and correct;

24. In case of suspected under declaration of gross sales/receipts, the Municipal Treasurer or his duly authorized representative to conduct audit or examine/assess the books of accounts of the business taxpayers to determine the accurate gross sales or receipts of made which the tax maybe based and to prepare reports on the results of audit or examination of book of accounts;

25. Direct the BPLO and Municipal Treasure to maintain files on sworn statement of gross sales or receipts, audited FS, ITR, reports on results of audit or examination of accounts, or any evidence to support the declared gross sales or receipts of the business taxpayers; and
26. Consider imposing sanctions to business establishments in committing false attestation in declaration of gross receipts or sales.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

27. Intensify the collection of delinquent RPT by frequently notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law, and regular conduct IEC on RPT payment in collaboration with the barangay officials.
28. Regularly send List of RPT Delinquency to the Provincial Treasurer for information and enforcement of civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

33. Provide remarks as “Found at Station” and inclusion of non-existing” or “missing” PPEs in the RPCPPE;
34. Keep record of the alternative activities/procedures undertaken and the reasons for the deviation from the PIP.
35. Identify the cause for any variance between RPCPPE and accounting records;
36. Complete the prescribed procedures under COA Circular 2020-06 with regard to items found at station and non-missing PPEs;
37. Prepare the IIRUP for identified unserviceable items;

38. Direct Property unit to issue demand letters to those accountable officers for missing PPEs established during the physical count, and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine the responsible persons for the loss, among others; and

39. Direct the Property and Accounting Units to undertake collaborative reconciliation procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their corresponding records which includes among others, the maintenance of their respective PC in appropriate forms.

Furthermore, with regard to the identified unrecorded donated PPEs, we recommended and the LCE agreed to:

40. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to secure copies of documents and other pertinent records of the donated assets to support the recording in the books of account of the Municipal Accountant;
41. Direct the GSO to provide property stickers and PC for monitoring of custody and accountability; and

42. Create appraisal committee for the valuation of the assets in case documents for its measurement are not successfully obtained from the donor LGU especially those assets with Certificate of Registration issued by the Land Transportation Office.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the: 
43. GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete LRNPC for all roads and its components, at the end of the accounting period, render a Report of Local Road Network (RLRN) of the LGU concerned.

45. We recommended and the Management agreed to follow-up the processing for the issuance of the Certificate of Title in favor of the Municipality and create a committee for the purpose of ensuring that real properties of the Municipality are covered by a certificate of title.

46. We further recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review its Land account securing sufficient bases on any recorded amount and adjust or include the value of real properties with issued title.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
  
47. Conduct gender audit using Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; and

48. Equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD by undergoing/attending trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs.
49. We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs identified in the GPB.

1.9  We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of delinquent taxpayers along with the corresponding unpaid taxes due; and
The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the certified list, draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the total tax receivables, as follows:
General Fund (GF):
Dr: RPT Receivables 				P3,854,887.39
Cr: Deferred RPT					P3,854,887.39
Special Education Fund (SEF):
Dr: SET Receivables				P3,854,887.39
Cr: Deferred SET					P3,854,887.39
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to establish the correct balance of unpaid taxes due in CY 2024 and adjust the discrepancies accordingly.

1.17 We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding Deferred RPT and SET since collections from advance payments of RPT/SET pertained to upcoming years; thus, cannot be offset in the current year RPT/SET Receivables, as shown below:
Dr: RPT Receivable			P1,214,816.60
Cr: Deferred RPT				P1,214,816.60
Dr: SET Receivable			P1,214,816.50
Cr: Deferred SET				P1,214,816.50

We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The HRMO, during annual budget preparations, to include those employees/officials who will retire and whose term of service will end in the ensuing year in the computation of the terminal leave benefits.
The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to prioritize the allocation of leave benefits computed by the HRMO in the Annual Budget.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable and subsequently, the leave benefits that are expected to be settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period. The proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Dr: Prior Period Adjustments			P3,975,350.28
Cr: Leave Benefits Payable			P3,975,350.28
To recognize prior years’ accumulated earned leave credits
as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
Leave Benefits Payable
Terminal Leave Benefits*
Prior Period Adjustments**
Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
** PYs’ leave benefits due to increase of salary in CY 2025;
Ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and IPSAS 20 by providing the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement, specifically on the aggregate remuneration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those who are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and
Provide disclosure in the Notes to FS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year.

3.5 We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to meticulously review the JEV and ensure to classify each expenses related to meals and snacks depending on the activity it pertains to be guided by the prescribed RCALGU.

Recommendations
4.12 We recommended, and Management agreed to issue a memorandum to all end-users/department that any donation received is to be reported to the Municipal Accountant, duly accompanied by a PTR or any relevant documents.
4.13 We also recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Record all identified donated medical equipment in the books of accounts, using verified PTRs as the basis for determining asset value. This ensures that the donation is properly recognized in compliance with accounting standards. The suggested adjusting entry is as follows:
Dr: Medical Equipment				P1,755,546.02
Cr: Prior Year’s Adjustment 			P1,755,546.02;
Recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation for the donated Medical Equipment to reflect the accurate net book value in the FS for CY 2024. The suggested adjusting entry is as follows:
Dr: Prior Year’s Adjustment (Income)			P138,038.48
Cr: Accumulated Depreciation – Medical Equipment 	P138,038.48;
Disclose all relevant details regarding donated PPE items in the Notes to FS;
Request a copy of the PTR and/or relevant documents from the source agencies on the donated tractors, constructed evacuation and operation centers of LDRRM Office and other PPE enumerated in Appendix G;and
Upon receipt of PTR and/or any relevant documents of donated PPE, draw a JEV including its depreciation, for recording in the appropriate PPE accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of the PPE account of the Municipality, specifically, the following:
The LCE to temporarily relieve the members of the Inventory Committee of all their regular duties to be able to complete the physical inventory of all PPEs of the LGU as required.
Inventory Committee to –
Prepare an updated PIP, and submit it to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the conduct of inventory-taking is properly and adequately planned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Conduct a complete physical count of PPE based on the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
Ensure that the physical count is recorded/documented in the standard ICF; and
Prepare the RPCPPE after the physical count based on the ICF.
The GSO  to prepare and update property records based on the results of the physical inventory and reconcile them with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered the correct balance of the LGU's PPEs.
Municipal Accountant to record adjustments after the completion of proper reconciliation to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, supported by appropriate documents.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The Inspectorate Team, under the Municipal Engineering Office, to conduct a more rigorous inspection and acceptance process to ensure that all work items listed in the POW are thoroughly verified before certifying project completion. This should involve a comprehensive checklist aligned with the POW and the requirements set forth in paragraph 2 of item (i) of paragraph 3 of Annex “A” of the Detailed Engineering for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects under the updated 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184, ensuring that each phase is fully usable and structurally sound before acceptance.
The Municipal Engineer to require that any unfinished items listed in the POW be promptly completed before certifying phase completion. In cases where partial completion is necessary, a clear delineation of the usable portion must be established, consistent with Annex “A” standards. Any delays or deferrals should be documented, with justifications reviewed and approved by the project oversight team to ensure transparency and accountability.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Ensure that the BAC to conduct other alternative procedures to lure out other responsive bidders to generate wider competition in order to obtain the most advantageous price for the LGU; and
The BAC to craft policies and sanctions to prevent nuisance/uninterested bidders that may undermine or even rig the results of the procurement process.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC strictly comply with the requirements and procedures for SVP as provided in Annex H of the RIRR of RA 9184 in its succeeding procurement of goods and materials by sending the RFQs to at least three qualified suppliers to ensure that the most advantageous prices and terms for the procured goods and materials are obtained.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to consider the revision of BAC Resolution No. 48, series of 2019 by excluding the imposition of a fee for the sale of quotation documents for procurements through SVP or any alternative mode of procurement, to encourage suppliers to respond/participate in the invitation/request made and ultimately to obtain the best price and quality of the items procured.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MDRRM Officer utilize the five per cent LDRRMF appropriations accordingly;
Refrain from charging against the 5 per cent LDRRMF for the operational and administrative expenses of the MDRRM Office; and
The MDDRM Officer propose a budget to the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Budget Officer, for the operational and administrative expenses of the Office under the GF.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to exclude augmentations and supplemental appropriations that cause the increase in the available balance of allowable MOOE items and that no succeeding augmentations and supplemental appropriations are to be allowed to cover obligations incurred after the establishment of the available balance of allowable MOOE for CNA Incentives and to ensure that savings from PS, other non-allowable MOOE items,continuing appropriations-CO, and surplus from previous years are not used as a funding source for CNA.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that the MDRRM Council, before revision is to be made to the MDRRMFIP, obtain first the available unobligated balance of each PPAs from the Municipal Budget Officer, determine the savings and obtain approval from the SB for the reversion of savings in order to determine the amount to be realigned to new or existing PPAs to avoid inaccurate Revised MDRRMFIP.
Likewise, the Municipal Budget Officer, being a member of the MDRRM Council, ensure that the available unobligated balances of each PPAs/savings to be reverted to the GF are already determined prior to the preparation of the Revised MDRRMFIP.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Disposal Committee, following the procedures set forth in COA-DBM JC No. 2024-1:
Fast track the conduct of a public auction for the construction and heavy equipment; and
Upon receipt of the IIRUP, conduct appraisal and disposal of other unserviceable PPEs.
The Inventory Committee complete the conduct of a physical inventory of PPE and prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPE; and
The GSO prepare the IIRUP based on the RPCPPE, using the prescribed format for all PPE found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed, and submit it to the Disposal Committee for appraisal and disposal.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Intensify the collection of delinquent taxes on real property by frequently notifying delinquent taxpayers through the issuance of notices and regular updates of postings of Notices of Delinquency on the LGU bulletin board and in conspicuous places in each barangay concerned; and
Prioritize the conduct of IEC on RPT to educate taxpayers on the importance and benefits of paying their respective tax dues on time and encourage delinquent taxpayers to avail the tax amnesty on real property taxes and special levies on real property per RA No. 12001 dated June 13, 2024.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) agreed to conduct continuous monitoring of the implementation of GAD PPAs identified in the GPB in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the GFPS agreed in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
Update the GAD Database by conducting gender analysis to identify gender issues/gaps and determine gender-responsive PPAs for prioritization in programming and budgeting in the annual GPB in accordance with Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and
Ensure that proposed GAD PPAs of departments for allocation are supported by a program profile containing the identified gender issues/GAD mandates and the gender analysis situation of the community.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the following concerned parties agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly reports and submit to the LSB prior the deadline for submission to other concerned Offices/Departments;
The LSB:
Submit to DepEd CO and furnish a copy to the Sanggunian concerned, and Regional Offices of DBM and DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter, and for the annual report not later than the 15th day of February of the following year; and
Post the quarterly SEF Utilization report in the three identified conspicuous places not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to properly classify the balance of Flood Control System and Road Networks:

Dr. Road Networks P16,902,205.13
Cr. Flood Control System P16,902,205.13
2. Moving forward, strengthen review of the transactions and properly record the transactions to its correct account classification.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:

3. Municipal Treasurer furnish RPT Account Register to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year.
4. Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the RPT Account Register, draw a JEV to set-up the RPT /SET Receivables and its Deferred RPT/SET, in its respective books of accounts.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal accountant to reclassify the balance of the Due to NGA account to the Due to GOCC account:

Dr. Due to NGA      P2,689,705.47
Cr. Due to GOCC           P2,689,705.47

Therefore, in view of the accrual basis of accounting, this Office recommended, and Management agreed that:

6. The Municipal Health Officer to regularly communicate and submit to the Municipal Accountant a Monthly List of Reimbursable Claims filed with PHIC.

The Municipal Accountant to:

7. Record in the GF books of accounts the PHIC reimbursable claims from PHIC upon receipt of the List from the RHU by a debit to the Due from GOCC-PHIC account and credit to Revenue accounts and refrain from accounting the receipt of payments from PHIC as TF; and

8. Make representation to the Philhealth that the LGU shall follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received from Philhealth for services rendered to patients with Philhealth coverage.
12. To create an Investigation Committee and perform the task mentioned in Item 7.2 of the COA Circular; and
14. We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC to send invitation to observers to include other stages of the procurement process as mentioned in Section 13.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
15. We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC shall invite observers at least five calendar days before the procurement stage or activity

We recommended, and Management agreed that:

16. The LDC through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that projects to be prioritized and implemented out of the 20 per cent DF are properly planned, and procurement-and-implementation-ready.

The Municipal Engineer to:

17. Fast-track the coordination with appropriate national agencies for immediate resolution of issues on lots; and
18. Establish monitoring process to ensure that all projects under the 20 per cent DF are timely implemented.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

19. Cease the practice on charging ineligible expenditures from LDRRMF; and
20. Allocate a separate budget in the GF for the MDRRM Office intended for its operational expenses.

21. Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF as at year-end and not merely as set-up of obligation.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
 
22. Thru the Municipal Treasurer, to propose an amendment to Revenue Ordinance to require the submission of audited FS and/or monthly, quarterly or annual ITR of the preceding taxable year to provide a more reliable basis for assessment of business taxes;

29. We recommended, and Management agreed to create and establish Monitoring and Evaluation Team in consonance with the provision Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate the LGU GAD PPAs.

30. We recommended, and Management agreed that the GFPS thru the Municipal Mayor endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, we recommend that the ordinance include provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the:

31. Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV upon receipt of paid DVs and continuously communicate with the Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs and its supporting documents within the prescribed period.
32. Municipal Treasurer to regularly notify the payees of their checks to ensure timely release from their Office and submission to the Municipal Accountant of paid DVs.

44. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to fully institutionalize the LDRRM Office by filling up the statutory/ mandatory positions to assist the DRRM Officer in the administration and training and research and planning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

50. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to fast-track the segregation of solid waste by conducting information drive at the barangay level to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

51. We recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 37 of RA No. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing open dump site.

52. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, information and education campaign to be regularly conducted for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.

24.	We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to fully institutionalize the LDRRM Office by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the DRRM Officer in the administration and training and research and planning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

24.	We recommended and the Management agreed to follow-up the processing for the issuance of the Certificate of Title in favor of the Municipality and create a committee for the purpose of ensuring that real properties of the Municipality are covered by a certificate of title.

25.	We further recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review its Land account securing sufficient bases on any recorded amount and adjust or include the value of real properties with issued title.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
       
26.	Conduct gender audit using Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; and

27.	Equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD by undergoing/attending trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs.
28.	We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs identified in the GPB.

31.	We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to fast-track the segregation of solid waste by conducting information drive at the barangay level to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

32.	We recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 37 of RA No. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing open dump site.

33.	We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, information and education campaign to be regularly conducted for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.
15. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to review the JEV before approving the same to ensure the correct accounts to be used in recording transactions in the FS.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to:

16.	Stop the practice of charging against the 20 per cent DF the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development; and

17.	Strictly observe the guidelines provided by the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 per cent EDF.

We recommended and Management agreed:

18.	To revert the excess appropriation/allotments from completed PPAs to other viable PPAs thru Supplemental Budget.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

19.	Require the SB that authorization for realignments of funds be made through the passage of an Appropriation Ordinance as prescribed under Sections 321, 322 and 336 of RA No. 7160, and submit to the SP for review; and

20.	Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no disbursements shall be made from realigned funds unless supported with an Appropriation Ordinance.

21.	We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.
23.	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure complete documentation before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.

29.	Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed that the GFPS prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with PCW MC No. 2018-04 and be made as basis in the preparation of the succeeding year’s GPB.

30.	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unremitted contributions and to regularly remit the monthly premiums of individual employees as provided in the above cited regulations to avoid interest and penalties for delayed remittance.

MT to mark the stale checks cancelled on its face.  For those unclaimed stale checks with original DV and related SDs are still with the former, the cancelled checks be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report.  The original DV and SDs be returned to the MA for the preparation of a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable. For those stale checks in the hand of the payee, the same be reported in the RCI by the MT and be recorded as Accounts Payable by the Municipal Accountant.
MA to prepare the separate schedule with aging for the relevant accounts.

MA to investigate/trace the causes/reasons of the negative balances of the payable accounts. Effect the adjustment in the agency’s books of account when there are necessary adjusting entries to be made.

Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory taking for CY 2021 in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006, prepare the RPCPPE using the prescribed form and to include those PPEs not included in the list provided by the Municipal Accountant, after the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, reconciliation thereof shall be made with that of the accounting unit’s and GSO’s reports to establish reliability and fair valuation of the PPE accounts within ten (10) days from the submission of completed RPCPPE, moreover, those missing PPEs, if there is any, established during the physical count, demand letters are to be issued to those accountable officers and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others and the GSO Designate to maintain property card and ledger cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.
MA to prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit to the COA Auditor thru the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month.

Management to fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the DRRM Officer in the administration and training and research and planning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

Municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; To equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD, they need to undergo trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PPAs; and revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9710 and other related provisions of law.
Management to fast-track the segregation of solid waste to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

To enhance the operation by management regarding the discussed conditions, we recommended that the concerned offices implement control measures necessary for the timely transmittal of documents to allow earlier preparation of reports and timely submission to this Office.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:

            1. The Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE)  to  create  an  Inventory
               Committee which includes at least one member  each  from  the
               Accounting and Property Division/Units of the Agency.


            2. The LCE to relieve  the  personnel  who  were  designated  as
               Inventory Committee members  of  their  present  and  regular
               duties to devote their full time in  executing  the  specific
               guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing  of  PPE
               account balances of the LGU.


            3. Each office of the LGU to fill up the ICF and submit the same
               to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then  be
               used by the Inventory Committee as basis in  the  preparation
               of its PIP and will be considered as part of their  inventory
               working paper.


            4. The Inventory Committee to prepare a PIP in  accordance  with
               the guidelines.


            5. Moreover, the  Inventory  Committee  to  record/document  the
               physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major  account
               group and will be the basis  in  the  preparation  of  RPCPPE
               after the physical count.


            6. The Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs Found at  Station
               and  List  of  Non-Existing/Missing  PPEs   during   physical
               inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting  Unit  for
               recording in the books of accounts of the  LGU  to  establish
               the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.


            7. Property unit to issue demand letters  to  those  accountable
               officers  for  those  missing  PPEs  established  during  the
               physical count and cause the conduct of an  investigation  to
               determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.


            8. The Property and Accounting Units to undertake  collaborative
               reconciliation procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in
               the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their  corresponding  records
               which  includes  among  others,  the  maintenance  of   their
               respective PCs.


            9. The LCE to ensure that the guidelines and procedures  in  the
               conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA  Circular
               2020-006 are strictly observed  to  facilitate  the  one-time
               cleansing of PPE account balances.

   Management comment:

    25. Management agreed with the audit observation and promised to comply
        with the audit recommendations.

16. We recommended the LCE to enjoin the  following  department  heads'
        compliance with the requirements of the Circular:

           1. MA to prepare the LRNLC, keep and maintain subsidiary records
              for roads and road components for every road network, prepare
              a lapsing schedule for the computation  of  the  depreciation
              for each component at the end of  the  year  and  review  the
              disclosure made in the NTFS of the LGU;


           2. GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map,  keep  a
              complete LRNPC for all roads and its components, and  at  the
              end of the accounting period, render RLRN of the LGU;


           3.  ME  to  provide  the  MA  and  the  GSO  with  the  complete
              description and cost segregation of road components for  road
              projects; and

           4. The Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCRN of the LGU.



   Management comment:


    17. Management agreed with the audit observation and promised to comply
        with the audit recommendations.

6. We recommended the LCE requires the MA to review  the  schedule  or
        records of PPE accounts and prepare the necessary correcting  entry
        to  adjust  the  PPE  account   taking   into   consideration   the
        capitalization threshold of P15,000 for recognition as provided  in
        Section 4 of the Circular.

   Management comment:


   7. Management agreed with the audit observation and  promised  to  comply
      with the audit recommendations.

14. We recommended that Management direct the MA to prepare the BRS  of
        all bank accounts of the municipality on a timely basis  and  avoid
        further delay and submit to this Office. Such reconciliation, is to
        be accompanied with the bank statements or bank snapshots  together
        with the negotiated or cleared checks, direct the MT and MA to make
        the necessary  adjustment  in  their  respective  records  for  the
        variances noted, direct MT to mark the stale  checks  cancelled  on
        its face.  For those unclaimed stale checks with  original  DV  and
        related SDs are still with the  former,  the  cancelled  checks  be
        presented in the RCI after the last check  issued  for  the  period
        indicated in the report.   The  original  DV  and  SDs  are  to  be
        returned to the MA for the preparation  of  a  JEV  to  record  the
        transaction as Accounts Payable.  For those  stale  checks  in  the
        hand of the payee, the same be reported in the RCI by the MT and be
        recorded as Accounts Payable; and direct the  MT  to  send  written
        notification to the payee of the existence of the check  one  month
        prior it will become stale.








   Management comment:


    15. Management agreed with the audit observation and promised to comply
        with the audit recommendations.

6. We recommended  that  Management  direct  the  MA  to  only  record
        liabilities if the goods were already received or the services were
        already rendered by the supplier or contractor  on  or  before  the
        reporting date and to disclose in the NTFS all valid commitments at
        year-end, moving forward.

   Management comment:


     7. Management agreed with the audit observation and promised to comply
        with the audit recommendations.

4. We  recommended  that  Management  direct  the  MA  to  follow  the
        illustrative accounting entries in the Annex C of COA Circular  No.
        2012-002.


   Management comment:


     5. Management agreed with the audit observation and promised to comply
        with the audit recommendations.

6.  We  recommended  that  Management  require  the   MA   to   record
        transactions and  use  appropriate  accounts  consistent  with  COA
        Circular No.2015-009.



   Management comment:


     7. Management agreed with the audit observation and promised to comply
        with the audit recommendations.

BAC to observe the procurement procedures for Negotiated Procurement through Emergency cases under Section 53.2 and 53.9 and ensure that the RFQs provide for the prescribed period for submission of documentary requirements by the suppliers.

Distribution team to revisit their distribution process to be able to address the conditions noted above and to institutionalize internal control mechanism to correctly record and report the items and quantity distributed to the beneficiaries.

Management to direct all accountable officers who were granted petty cash to liquidate and return the unused cash at year end to the MT, who will issue an official receipt to acknowledge the returned cash.

Municipal Budget Officer to record the liquidation and cancellation of the obligation request to its appropriate registries.

MA to evaluate the stage of implementation of each of the government programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) related to these funds recorded in Due to NGA’s account under the TF.  If the purpose to which it was given was already complied or completed, the said funds should be either returned it to the source agency or considered as income by the agency in accordance with the provisions in the agreement or contract.  However, if upon evaluation, the said PPA is still on going, management should fast track its implementation as these funds are with the agency for more than five years.

For the other liability accounts, MA to evaluate the relevant liability accounts and prepare adjusting entry to revert it to unappropriated surplus which were over two years or more and without valid actual claims supported with Sangguniang Bayan resolution in consonance with Sec. 98 of PD No. 1445.

Management to provide the necessary additional support on the stairways and to remove any obstruction on both ends of each stairway/ladder for the fast, convenient, and safe escape of occupants in case of fire or other emergency.

Management to ensure that all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. Management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS. Likewise, furnish this Office the required Property Inventory Form not later than April 30 each year.

The Municipality file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances together with the required supporting documents, which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to the above-mentioned regulation.
Management may institute appropriate actions against the NGO/PO who violated the provisions contained in the approved MOA.

Improve the operation of the Municipality particularly in keeping of records to support the information reported in the financial statements, it is recommended:

That the Municipal Mayor  ensure that the Municipal Inventory Committee shall conduct the annual physical count of the PPE, to prepare and submit the RPCPPE and RPCI to the Auditor, reconcile the information gathered during the conduct of inventory taking with the information maintained by the Municipal Accountant, investigate the variance, and make appropriate action to address the results of the investigation; and

That the Municipal Accountant and GSO – Designate prepare and maintain the required cards and ledger cards.

Management shall insure all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. It is also recommended for management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS.
The Municipal Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

Management hasten the completion and optimize the utilization of the fund by fast-tracking the project implementation and review those that may no longer be the utmost priority of the Municipality and reprogram the same to other more important PPAs economically beneficial to its constituents.
The MDRRMO prepare and submit the required LDRRMFIF.

Management to:

Conduct IEC campaign about proper ESWM and disseminate the approved local ordinance to the public; and

Strictly implement the approved local ordinances related to ESWM.

We recommended that management complete the physical count, prepare properly the RPCPPE by accomplishing all columns in the form and indicating the whereabouts, condition and other relevant information relative to property, plant and equipment and submit the report within the prescribed period, and regularly reconcile with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), General Ledger and Inventory Report including other property records like the updated Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE).
We recommended management to provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

We recommended that the management to issue demand letters to those with outstanding cash advances for its full settlement and liquidations, to deduct from the salary to those with outstanding cash advances; and for those dormant accounts involving 10 years and above, require the filing of request for authority to write-off.

We recommended that management institute appropriate action against the NGO/PO by blacklisting the latter and prohibit them from participating in future undertakings provided by the municipality. Meanwhile, conduct an inspection and appraisal on vehicle to be turnover and consider its recognition in the books of accounts at fair value.

We reiterated our recommendation that management insure all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. We also recommended for management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS. Likewise, furnish this office the required Property Inventory Form not later than April 30 each year.

We recommended that management closely monitor project implementation to ensure the completion of developmental projects to benefit their constituents, identify and prioritize development projects that could be implemented during the year taking into consideration the technical manpower of the Municipality, consider changes in the budget for projects which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan, and consider the hiring of a technical person, preferably a civil engineer,  to complement the inadequate personnel in the Municipal Engineering Office,  purposely in the preparation of the detailed estimates and engineering plans of identified infrastructure projects and support its implementation.
We reiterated our recommendation that the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the required LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that management to fully institutionalize the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Office by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the DRRM Officer in the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning to assure implementation of plan of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the management exert extra effort in installing/maintaining a system of tax mapping by considering the hiring of additional personnel with expertise on real property field assessment to assists in reviewing all tax mapping documents to properly identify location, area, improvements, ownership and index number of properties.

We recommended that management fast-track the segregation of solid waste to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

We reiterated our recommendation that the municipality to impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, information and education campaign to be regularly conducted for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.

We recommended that management to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; to equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD, they need to undergo trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9710 and other related provisions of law.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the BFP, re-conduct evaluation and inspection on the municipal buildings’ fire safety standards and implement fire preventive measures like the creation of permanent stairways and/or improvise ladder to avoid the risk of losing lives and properties in case of man-made and/or natural fire calamities, to provide firefighting equipment not only limited to fire sprinkler and fire alarm at the conspicuous places and formulate post emergency evacuation plan where anyone can have access, and remove all obstruction on passage ways/aisles and ensure that no hindrances exist on its fire exits, in compliance to the above cited provisions.

The Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/ stale checks specifically those checks issued prior to CY 2018. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the former with relevant documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

	It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the noted variance and make an appropriate action to correct it.
The Municipal Accountant fast track the review and make the appropriate adjustment to the books of accounts of the Municipality, if necessary.
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries.

The Municipal Accountant provide depreciation expenses to all depreciable properties following the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2003-007 and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts to reflect the correct carrying value of the PPE.

The Municipal Accountant will not provide for residual value in the computation of depreciation for road network and prepare an adjusting entry by debiting Government Equity account and crediting Accumulated Depreciation – Road Networks by P0.878 million.

The Municipality to enhance its compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:

Requiring the submission of the report of the Local Road Network by the GSO - Designate in order to implement and monitor the recognition of the account Local Road Network in the books of accounts;

Making proper disclosure in the Financial Statement of Road Network System; and

Exercising proper reconciliation between Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and make the necessary adjustment.
The Municipal Accountant make appropriate adjusting entries to reclassify the affected accounts including its corresponding accumulated depreciation accounts.

Management shall approved disbursements with complete supporting documents. Personnel in-charge of performing pre-audit activities must be directed to carefully ascertain completeness of the required supporting documents before any claim is processed for payment.
The Management refrain from paying regular expenses from the PCF.
The Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by instituting collection strategies.
The management provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste as required under RA No. 9003.

1) Coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by the Municipality.

2) Reconciliation should be done of the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the MPDO in the preparation of PPAs report before submission to COA for audit validation.

3) Require the concerned official/ Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and

4) Submit updated PPA report if there are corrections, revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for validation and monitoring.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality informed that they will also conduct inventory on its Road Network components and will comply with the reportorial requirements in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management of the Municipality should insure all insurable assets with the GSIS to cover for the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event, and to comply with  Section 489 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 . Furthermore, the Municipality should include in the municipal budget the necessary funds for the payment of insurance premiums for the properties for CY 2018 onwards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality informed that they will accelerate the processing of the insurance coverage of its insurable assets. The Municipality further informed that the process of application of insurance coverage in the GSIS takes time; this is the reason why no insurance coverage was issued on the Municipality’s insurable assets.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks issued prior to CY 2018. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the former with relevant documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal to reconcile the noted variance and make an appropriate action to correct it.

It is recommended that the Municipality shall file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances together with the required supporting documents, which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to the above-mentioned regulation.
It is recommended that management may institute appropriate actions against the NGO/PO who violated the provisions contained in the approved MOA.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast track the review and make the appropriate adjustment to the books of accounts of the Municipality, if necessary.

To address the conditions noted above and to improve the operation of the Municipality particularly in keeping of records to support the information reported in the financial statements, it is recommended:
That the Municipal Mayor shall ensure that the Municipal Inventory Committee shall conduct the annual physical count of the PPE, to prepare and submit the RPCPPE and RPCI to the Auditor, reconcile the information gathered during the conduct of inventory taking with the information maintained by the Municipal Accountant, investigate the variance, and make appropriate action to address the results of the investigation; and
That the Municipal Accountant and GSO – Designate shall prepare and maintain the required cards and ledger cards.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expenses to all depreciable properties following the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2003-007 and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts to reflect the correct carrying value of the PPE.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant will not provide for residual value in the computation of depreciation for road network and prepare an adjusting entry by debiting Government Equity account and crediting Accumulated Depreciation – Road Networks by P877,571.99.

It is recommended for the Municipality to enhance its compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:
Requiring the submission of the report of the Local Road Network by the GSO - Designate in order to implement and monitor the recognition of the account Local Road Network in the books of accounts;
Making proper disclosure in the Financial Statement of Road Network System; and
Exercising proper reconciliation between Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and make the necessary adjustment.

It is recommended that management shall insure all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. It is also recommended for management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make appropriate adjusting entries to reclassify the affected accounts including its corresponding accumulated depreciation accounts.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

It is recommended that management hasten the completion and optimize the utilization of the fund by fast-tracking the project implementation and review those that may no longer be the utmost priority of the Municipality and reprogram the same to other more important PPAs economically beneficial to its constituents.
It is recommended that the MDRRMO will prepare and submit the required LDRRMFIF.

It is recommended that management shall approved disbursements with complete supporting documents. Personnel in-charge of performing pre-audit activities must be directed to carefully ascertain completeness of the required supporting documents before any claim is processed for payment.
We recommended that the Management shall refrain from paying regular expenses from the Petty Cash Fund.
It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by instituting collection strategies.

It is recommended that management shall:
Conduct IEC campaign about proper ESWM and disseminate the approved local ordinance to the public; and
Strictly implement the approved local ordinances related to ESWM.
We recommended that the management shall provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste as required under RA No. 9003.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality informed that they already closed the PNB Liloy Branch account in the 1st quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality should file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should deposit his collection intact.


The Municipal Treasurer should comply with the preparation and submission of MRAAF. Collectors should be required to submit MRAAF individually which is the basis of the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the consolidated MRAAF. And no accountable forms should be issued to a collector if accountable forms in his custody are not liquidated or reported as collection with the preparation of the corresponding Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD).

The Municipal Treasurer should implement the guidelines in the remittance of collection, monitor the frequency of remittance of collectors and tellers and institute control measures in the operation of the Office.

The Municipal Treasurer should use as basis the consolidated RCD prepared by the Liquidating Officer. Observe the submission, retention and filing of RCDs to improve the internal control of collection and availability of records for monitoring and reconciliation.

The Municipal Treasurer should monitor and institute control mechanism in the remittance of collection. Intensify the monitoring of accountable forms issued to collectors and on stock and avoid the issuance of accountable forms to collectors who are still in possession of substantial pieces of accountable forms until it will be remitted or a proper accounting thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should not act as collecting officer. All collections should be collected by the collectors to strengthen its internal control and accountability mechanism in cash management.

We recommend that collectors should retained copies of their submitted RCD’s for reference and evidence of their remittances.

We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should rotate its collector especially the field collectors. Institute control mechanism in the assignment of collector.

We recommend that there should be a 24 hour security service especially assigned in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipality should consider installation of CCTV cameras inside the Office and premises of the Municipal Treasurer and the whole municipal building.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should be guided with the NGAS Manual in maintaining/recording transactions in the cash book.

Coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by the Municipality.

Reconciliation should be done of the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the MPDO in the preparation of PPAs report before submission to COA for audit validation.

Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and

Submit updated PPA report if there are corrections, revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for validation and monitoring.
observations.reason:
Lack of personnel in the office of the Municipal Engineer to do the task of reporting.
 The municipality will comply with the recommendation in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommend  that  the  Municipality  enhance  its  compliance  with
policies and procedures in:

       a. Completing the actual physical count and the RPCPPE and  reconcile
          the established differences to have accurate Fixed  Asset  records
          and reports for the Municipality for which it is held  accountable
          and  provide  adequate  disclosures  to   guide   the   users   of
          information;


       b. Maintaining proper Fixed  Asset  records  for  all  Municipality’s
          fixed assets;


       c. Assigning Fixed Asset to end user or the least the head of  office
          having custody of the assets to  facilitate  easy  monitoring  for
          inventory purposes and ensure that the same  are  being  kept  and
          safeguarded by responsible personnel




       d.  Updating information in the Fixed Asset records;


       e. Coordinating with different personnel/offices of the  Municipality
          in order to gather information as to  the  amount  of  acquisition
          cost and  the  date  of  acquisition  to  be  able  to  recognized
          depreciation expense.



          If no information is available, since in the actual physical count
          of  these  assets  they  are  classified  as  serviceable,  proper
          appraisal on the remaining useful life of the asset should be done
          and the corresponding depreciation expense shall be computed based
          on the  book – value as presented in the financial statements.


       f. Disposing unserviceable properties.

We recommend that the Municipal Government enhance its compliance with
policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:

        a. Requiring the submission of the report of the Local Road Network
           by the GSO/Municipal Treasurer in order to implement and monitor
           the recognition of the account Local Road Network in  the  books
           of accounts;


        b. Improving the maintenance of subsidiary ledger for the Road  and
           it components;


        c. Making proper disclosure in  the  Financial  Statement  of  Road
           Network System;

        d. Exercising proper reconciliation between Report on the  Physical
           Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network  Ledger  Card
           and make the necessary adjustment;


        e. Providing cost to specific Road Network in order to have  proper
           valuation and completeness of the Road Network Account.

1) Coordination must  be  established  between  the  Bids  and  Awards
         Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office  and  the
         Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation
         of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by  the
         Municipality.


      2) Reconciliation should be done of the records maintained by the BAC,
         Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the MPDO in  the
         preparation of PPAs report  before  submission  to  COA  for  audit
         validation.


      3) Require the  concerned  official/Municipal  Engineering  Office  to
         submit at  the  beginning  of  the  year  in  accordance  with  the
         provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No.  2013-004  dated  January  30,
         2013; and


      4) Submit updated PPA report if there are  corrections,  revisions  or
         changes to the  projects  to  be  implemented  for  validation  and
         monitoring.

We recommend that the management of the Municipality should insure all
insurable assets with the GSIS to cover for the cost of  the  properties  in
case of loss due to fire and other contingent  event,  and  to  comply  with

COA  Circular  No.  92-390  dated  November  17,  1992  .  Furthermore,  the

the payment of insurance premiums for the properties for CY 2018 onwards.

with  Philippine  National  Bank  and  transfer  the  amount  to  Government
Financial Institutions with universal bank license and a  CAMELS  rating  of
at least 3 in accordance with Section 5.2 of  Department  of  Finance  (DOF)
Department Circular (DC) No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015.

authority to write-off dormant receivables and  unliquidated  cash  advances
which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected  pursuant  to
COA Circular No 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  should   deposit   his
collection intact.

The  Municipal  Treasurer  should  comply  with  the  preparation  and
submission  of  MRAAF.  Collectors  should  be  required  to  submit   MRAAF
individually which is the basis of the Municipal Treasurer  to  prepare  the
consolidated  MRAAF.  And  no  accountable  forms  should  be  issued  to  a
collector if  accountable  forms  in  his  custody  are  not  liquidated  or
reported as collection with the preparation of the corresponding  Report  of
Collections and Deposits (RCD).

The Municipal  Treasurer  should  implement  the  guidelines  in  the
remittance of collection, monitor the frequency of remittance of  collectors
and tellers and institute control measures in the operation of the Office.

The Municipal Treasurer should use as  basis  the  consolidated  RCD
prepared  by the Liquidating Officer. Observe the submission, retention  and
filing  of  RCDs  to  improve  the  internal  control  of   collection   and
availability of records for monitoring and reconciliation.

The  Municipal  Treasurer  should  monitor  and  institute   control
mechanism in the remittance  of  collection.  Intensify  the  monitoring  of
accountable forms issued to collectors and on stock and avoid  the  issuance
of  accountable  forms  to  collectors  who  are  still  in  possession   of
substantial pieces of accountable forms until  it  will  be  remitted  or  a
proper accounting thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should not act as collecting
officer.  All  collections  should  be  collected  by  the   collectors   to
strengthen  its  internal  control  and  accountability  mechanism  in  cash
management.

We recommend that collectors should retained copies of their
submitted RCD’s for reference and evidence of their remittances.

We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should rotate
its collector especially the field collectors. Institute control mechanism
in the assignment of collector.

We recommend that there should be a 24 hour security service
especially assigned in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer. The
Municipality should consider installation of CCTV cameras inside the Office
and premises of the Municipal Treasurer and the whole municipal building.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should be guided with the
NGAS Manual in maintaining/recording transactions in the cash book.

           being kept and safeguarded by responsible personnel




        d.  Updating information in the Fixed Asset records;


        e.   Coordinating   with   different   personnel/offices   of   the
           Municipality in order to gather information as to the amount  of
           acquisition cost and the date  of  acquisition  to  be  able  to
           recognized depreciation expense.



           If no information is available, since  in  the  actual  physical
           count of these assets they are classified as serviceable, proper
           appraisal on the remaining useful life of the  asset  should  be
           done  and  the  corresponding  depreciation  expense  shall   be
           computed based  on  the   book  –  value  as  presented  in  the
           financial statements.


        f. Disposing unserviceable properties.

We recommend that  the  LSB  should  refrain  from  providing  non-
   priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for  those  that  are
   part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead,  it
   should be allocate SEF funds for the construction and  repair  of  school
   buildings; facilities and equipment; education research;  acquisition  of
   books and periodicals; and  sports  development.  Also,  the  LSB  should
   monitor   the   available    SEF    in    order    to    implement    the
   programs/projects/activities   in   a   timely   manner,   taking    into
   consideration the objectives set forth in RA 7160 and RA 5447.

Recommendation:

        We  recommend  that  management  should  close  the  bank   account
   maintained with Philippine National  Bank  and  transfer  the  amount  to
   Government Financial Institutions  with  universal  bank  license  and  a
   CAMELS rating of at least 3 in accordance with Section 5.2 of  Department
   of Finance (DOF) Department Circular (DC) No.  001-2015  dated  June  1,
   2015.

Recommendation:

        We recommend that the management of the Municipality should  insure
   all insurable assets  with  the  GSIS  to  cover  for  the  cost  of  the
   properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event, and to
   comply with  Section 489 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual
   Volume  I  and  COA  Circular  No.  92-390  dated  November  17,  1992  .
   Furthermore, the Municipality should include in the municipal budget  the
   necessary funds for the payment of insurance premiums for the  properties
   for CY 2018 onwards.

Recommendation:

        The Municipal Treasurer should  comply  with  the  preparation  and
   submission of MRAAF.  Collectors  should  be  required  to  submit  MRAAF
   individually which is the basis of the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the
   consolidated MRAAF. And no  accountable  forms  should  be  issued  to  a
   collector if accountable forms in  his  custody  are  not  liquidated  or
   reported as collection with the preparation of the  corresponding  Report
   of Collections and Deposits (RCD).

Recommendation:

     1. Coordination must  be  established  between  the  Bids  and  Awards
        Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office  and  the
        Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation
        of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by  the
        Municipality.


     2. Reconciliation should be done of the records maintained by the BAC,
        Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the MPDO in  the
        preparation of PPAs report  before  submission  to  COA  for  audit
        validation.

     3. Require the  concerned  official/Municipal  Engineering  Office  to
        submit at  the  beginning  of  the  year  in  accordance  with  the
        provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No.  2013-004  dated January  30,
        2013; and


     4. Submit updated PPA report if there are  corrections,  revisions  or
        changes to the  projects  to  be  implemented  for  validation  and
        monitoring.

The Municipal Mayor shall designate Property Officer so that the property and custodian function is not lodged with the Municipal Treasurer. Create an inventory committee who will complete the physical count and consider the designated Property Officer as the Chairman of the Committee. Likewise, direct the Inventory Committee to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property as basis for the accounting unit to recognize impairment losses and for its disposal. Further, require the designated Property Officer to update the stock and property cards maintained for inventories and property, plant and equipment. Reconciliation on their records is to be made by both the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend compliance of AO No. 70, s.2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of Internal Audit Service and its implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the guidelines on the organization and staffing of Internal Auditing Units.
